active_question: According to Rope365’s construction lesson, how many strands are most twisted ropes made from?
edited_answer: More plies generally make rope more robust but stiffer, while a tighter twist tends to make it more rigid and coarse and a looser twist smoother and more flexible; other construction factors can alter these tendencies.
edited_question: How does Rope365 say ply count and twist tightness can affect a rope’s handling?
paraphrase_rationale: The replacement teaches useful construction tradeoffs and their uncertainty instead of an isolated three-strand count.
reason: The replacement teaches useful construction tradeoffs and their uncertainty instead of an isolated three-strand count.
support_evidence: When shopping for rope, you might see a mention that rope is single-ply (1-ply) or double-ply (2-ply). This refers to the number of ply per yarn. Generally, a rope with more ply will be more robust, but also stiffer. Single-ply rope will generally be more flexible but will also require more maintenance and have a higher tendency to highstrand. But this is not always true. Other aspects of the construction can have a bigger impact on the rope characteristics. A tighter rope twist will make the rope more rigid with a more coarse texture while a lower twist will feel smoother and flexible.
